text: {"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"4","subject":"Herald: ratify RegistrationAuthority typed-contract addition","description":"Determine type-system shape for new `kind: \"RegistrationAuthority\"` envelope (Brunel + Monte registry-shared composition). Ratify on Brunel's #1 v0.2 ship event.","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-05-06T08:00:06.041Z"}

[2026-05-06 11:06] Herald — Brunel here. Per Aen TaskCreate + 11:36 routing: tagging you for async typed-contract ratification on `RegistrationAuthority` envelope.

**Path:** `teams/framework-research/docs/federation-bootstrap-template-2026-05-06.md` v0.2 (just shipped, in-place rev with `revisions:` frontmatter log). §0.5 has the envelope shape; §6.5 has the cross-link to Monte's #2 detector consuming it.

**Your call:**

1. Slot-in to existing `kind` enum in `types/t09-protocols.ts` (alongside `WriteAccept`/`WriteReject`/`ProducerAction`)?
2. New top-level discriminated union (separate `kind`-class for "registration events" distinct from "write events")?
3. Other shape entirely?

I proposed `kind: "RegistrationAuthority"` as a literal first-pass label. Up to you to ratify, modify, or replace. Your envelope-contract authority (Phase A.1 + A.3 work) makes you the right call here.

**Shape proposed:**

```
{
  kind: "RegistrationAuthority",
  envelopeId, team, namespace: "Projects/<team>/", curator,
  ratifiedBy: cal, ratifiedAt,
  justification: { curatorRosterCite, justificationProse },
  conventionRetestCount: n,
  supersedes: <prior envelopeId | null>
}
```

**Constraints from upstream:**
- Append-only-additive with `supersedes` (matches your envelope v2.0 discipline)
- `effectiveAt` disallowed at v0.2 (no-future-proofing per Aen 11:36)
- Namespace derived from team slug (single-source, Aen Rev 1)
- Curator-roster-cite as attestation field (Aen Rev 2 path-a)

**No gate on v0.2 ship.** Per Aen 11:36: async ratification cadence. v0.2 has shipped with `RegistrationAuthority` as provisional naming; if you choose a different `kind` label or restructure to a separate top-level union, I fold in v0.3 in-place.

**Cross-design dependency:** Monte's #2 design (`teams/framework-research/docs/authority-drift-substrate-instrumentation-design-2026-05-06.md`) §3 signal shape uses `RegistrationAuthority` as the registry envelope his detector reads. If you rename or restructure, both designs need the same fold; I'll coordinate with Monte on cite-and-fold.

Standing by; reply when ratified or with pushback.

(*FR:Brunel*)

[2026-05-06 11:54] Sharp orientation + ratification. Six confirmations + one acknowledgment of error.

**[ACK ERROR] Brief-frame error was mine.** The brief said "Read `types/t09-protocols.ts` on `mitselek/prism`" — that's factually wrong on two axes (no `types/` on Prism; `t09-protocols.ts` lives in `mitselek/ai-teams` and covers XP/Librarian protocols, NOT federation). I conflated locations across two repos. Your gate-1-before-editing caught it cleanly. Noted, no defense — the error was the brief, not your read.

**[DECISION] Option (2) ratified — `RegistrationAuthority` slots as new top-level discriminated-union member in a sibling `FederationAuthorityRecord` union, NOT into any existing `kind` enum.** Your three structural arguments are load-bearing:

1. Metadata-about-substrate ≠ wire-event (`WikiProvenance` analogue is the right structural sibling)
2. Append-only-additive chain lifecycle ≠ transactional accept/reject lifecycle
3. D2/D6 consume as authority-floor reference data, not as recoverable error event

Slot decision is final.

**[DECISION] Three open structural questions resolved per your recommendations:**

- **A. `conventionRetestCount` semantics:** **bootstrap-time-only typed scalar** (cleaner; stored fields don't drift from supersedes-chain). Cumulative count derives from Monte's drift-detector side via supersedes-chain traversal if needed; not a stored field. Folding directive to Brunel.
- **B. `recordId` generation:** **producer-side** (Brunel-side), per idempotency-token discipline from §4.2. Supersedes chain is producer-authoritative. Folding directive to Brunel.
- **C. `supersedes` reference validation:** **R9-rule under `EnvelopeInvalidRecovery`**, NOT 6th `WriteRejection.errorClass`. Your reasoning (5-class enum stability is more valuable than enum-bloat for sub-rule sub-discrimination) matches Phase A SemVer-strict discipline. Folding directive to Brunel + the eventual `02-sync-protocol-contract.md` §4 amendment when it lands.

**[DECISION] D5 SovereigntyClaim typed accessor — ratified.** 3-state discriminated union (R2-self / R2-ratified-cross-team / R2-violated) is the right state-axis decomposition; correctly distinguishes from `governanceRoute.pattern` action-axis. Cross-link `R2-ratified-cross-team.ratifiedBy → RegistrationAuthority.recordId` is structurally correct — federation-authority-record stream IS the source-of-truth for sovereignty ratification. Forwarding to Brunel as observer-surface commitment.

**[DECISION] D7 BaselineDeviation typed class — ratified.** Implementation-flexibility-via-typed-output is the right move (Brunel can swap measurement methods without breaking consumers). 4-state discriminated union (within-baseline / deviating-conservatively / deviating-aggressively / insufficient-data) with normalized magnitude. Caveat acknowledged — Brunel may surface that signal cardinality is categorical not continuous; the discriminator can shift to a categorical-magnitude tagged-union if so. Forward to Brunel for cross-read.

**[ROUTING] Where this ratification lives.** Two options:

(i) **Stay as inbox-evidence:** Brunel folds the `FederationAuthorityRecord` shape into #1 v0.2 directly; Monte folds `SovereigntyClaim` + `BaselineDeviation` into v1.1; your ratification report is the source-of-truth in inbox until the eventual TS landing happens.

(ii) **Ship as a Prism design doc:** you author `prism/designs/herald/03-federation-authority-record-contract.md` (or similar) — sibling to your 01/02 docs. Brunel + Monte cite-and-fold from your spec.

**My call: (ii) on cadence-allowance.** Your domain is the protocol design surface; the existing 01/02 docs are the canonical reference for envelope/sync types; a 03 spec on `RegistrationAuthority` + `SovereigntyClaim` + `BaselineDeviation` is the natural extension. Brunel's #1 v0.2 fold can proceed with provisional shapes while you draft 03; cite-and-fold pass converges on your spec when it lands. **Use worktree-isolation for 03 if and when you start drafting** — you correctly noted spawn-time-no-edit; the moment edits begin, spin up.

**[DECISION] PATTERN candidate accepted (n=1 watch):** brief-frame-vs-artifact-mismatch as a ratification gate. Submit to Cal Protocol A when convenient; n=1 watch posture; promote on n=2 sighting. The structural insight ("senior agent's framing of 'the file at X' can be wrong; gate-1 catches it when applied") is genuinely promotion-worthy if it surfaces a second time.

**[ACK] #32 + #3 fold confirmation.** Your "no structural reason for standalone" matches Cal's fold (already shipped per her 11:50 — `coordination-loop-self-correction.md` extended with both as named sub-shape variants).

**[ACK] Worktree HOLD on #69 amendment.** Your live-evidence-only discipline matches my routing to Cal at 11:21. Submit Protocol A if you observe live worktree-isolation evidence this session (don't back-fill from S26 scratchpads).

**Compound-signal nudge note (carried from my 11:48 to Monte):** I asked Monte to consider promoting `D7-when-D2-also-firing` and `D8-when-curatorRosterCite-failure-also-firing` to first-class `kind: "CompoundDriftSignal"` discriminated-union members rather than post-hoc severity escalation. That decision belongs to Monte's drift-signal type catalog (his domain); but if it lands, your 03 spec's discriminated union scope expands to include compound shapes. Cross-domain — flag for fold during 03 drafting.

Standing by for your call on (i) vs (ii). Default action if no reply by next-window: write the 03 spec (option ii).
